# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nodejs-microservice
spec:
  replicas: <number_of_replicas> # set this to the number of instances you want for your microservice, e.g., if it's a single instance use '1', and multiple is recommended using like "3" or more depending on how many services are running in parallel (like 5)
  selector:
    matchLabels: # This label will be used to select the pod(s). In this case, we set up for our microservice.
      app: nodejs-microservice  
  template:    
    metadata:      
        labels:        
          app : "nodejs_micorservice"          
    spec:             # Container specification in the pod definition (e.g., Dockerfile) is not included here, but it's done inside a PodTemplateSpec object within Deployment template 
      containers:      
        - name: node-env  
          image: <nodejs_image>    # replace with your NodeJS microservice docker Image URL or tag. e.g., "myNodeApp/nodeapp" : latest, myUserID/mongoDB:"latest", etc... 
      imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent     # This will prevent the container security from pulling images if they are not present in your registry (default is to always pull).   e.g., "Always","Never","IfNotPresent"
